Qubit Pharmaceuticals Announces Strategic Collaboration with Singapore's Centre for Quantum Technologies to Advance Quantum Algorithms for Drug Discovery
Two-year collaboration with CQT to develop and implement novel quantum algorithms for molecular discovery
Qubit Pharmaceuticals Announces Strategic Collaboration with Singapore's Centre for Quantum Technologies to Advance Quantum Algorithms for Drug Discovery
Two-year collaboration with CQT to develop and implement novel quantum algorithms for molecular discovery
Qubit Pharmaceuticals announces strategic collaboration with Singapore's Centre for Quantum Technologies to advance quantum algorithms for drug discovery.

By working with CQT and leveraging access to state-of-the-art quantum hardware, we aim to transition these algorithms from theoretical constructs into real computational tools for molecular discovery.
We are glad to partner with domain experts like Qubit Pharmaceuticals to show what quantum computers can do for problems people care about
Qubit Pharmaceuticals announces strategic collaboration with Singapore's Centre for Quantum Technologies to advance quantum algorithms for drug discovery.

By working with CQT and leveraging access to state-of-the-art quantum hardware, we aim to transition these algorithms from theoretical constructs into real computational tools for molecular discovery.
We are glad to partner with domain experts like Qubit Pharmaceuticals to show what quantum computers can do for problems people care about
The collaboration is led by Jean-Philip Piquemal at Qubit Pharmaceuticals and Sergi Ramos-Calderer at CQT, with an initial team of four researchers across both organisations.
The team has published details to the physics preprint server arXiv (<https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.08395>).
Qubit Pharmaceuticals announces strategic collaboration with Singapore's Centre for Quantum Technologies to advance quantum algorithms for drug discovery.

Our focus is implementing algorithms that can address real computational bottlenecks in chemistry. Working with CQT and Quantinuum hardware gives us an opportunity to evaluate these methods under realistic conditions and learn what is required to make them useful for molecular discovery.

Outperforms benchmarks: Beats current leading models in benchmarks relating to speed and can simulate molecular systems of a million atoms while maintaining quantum accuracy.
_copyright LCT - Sorbonne Université, CNRS_

pharma companies could use such models to predict drug binding and reactivity in the body with fewer approximations; materials scientists could explore catalytic reactions or battery electrolyte breakdown with chemical accuracy; chemical engineers could design processes knowing the simulations capture even subtle quantum effects; and in fundamental science, we might finally simulate things like enzyme catalysis or virus assembly with the same accuracy we currently reserve for tiny molecules.
Here quantum chemists, AI experts and molecular engineers are working hand in hand, whereas before they would struggle to speak each other's language.

occurrence_sentence: In April 2025, Sorbonne University and Qubit Pharmaceuticals researchers revealed FeNNix-Bio1, an AI foundation model that enables next-generation molecular simulations crucial to drug discovery.

interaction_phrase: Sorbonne University and Qubit Pharmaceuticals researchers revealed
occurrence_sentence: In April 2025, Sorbonne University and Qubit Pharmaceuticals researchers revealed FeNNix-Bio1, an AI foundation model that enables next-generation molecular simulations crucial to drug discovery.

interaction_phrase: Jean-Philip Piquemal, Qubit's Chief Scientific Officer
occurrence_sentence: "FeNNIx-Bio1 extends the breakthrough in protein structure prediction introduced by AlphaFold to any biomolecular simulation," says Jean-Philip Piquemal, Qubit's Chief Scientific Officer and the Distinguished Professor behind the FeNNix-Bio1 team.

"FeNNIx-Bio1 extends the breakthrough in protein structure prediction introduced by AlphaFold to any biomolecular simulation," says Jean-Philip Piquemal, Qubit's Chief Scientific Officer and the Distinguished Professor behind the FeNNix-Bio1 team.
"This unbiased approach does not focus on what has been previously discovered. Instead, it learns how molecules should behave, based on the underlying physics and chemistry laws," Marino adds.

"This unbiased approach does not focus on what has been previously discovered. Instead, it learns how molecules should behave, based on the underlying physics and chemistry laws, " Marino adds. "At the same time, it's flexible—evolve the building blocks, and you can model just about anything."

interaction_phrase: a breakthrough from Qubit Pharmaceuticals and Pasqal
occurrence_sentence: That's where a breakthrough from Qubit Pharmaceuticals and Pasqal comes in.

interaction_phrase: have built the most accurate synthetic database ever for molecular chemistry
occurrence_sentence: Sorbonne and Qubit have built the most accurate synthetic database ever for molecular chemistry, leveraging powerful computing resources from GENCI, EuroHPC, and Argonne National Laboratory.

interaction_phrase: unveil the world's most powerful Quantum AI model
occurrence_sentence: Sorbonne University and Qubit Pharmaceuticals unveil the world's most powerful Quantum AI model to discover new medicines

The quantum AI model, developed in partnership with Sorbonne University, is capable of modeling and simulating the behavior of molecules with a level of precision and computational speed never before achieved.
Jean-Philip Piquemal, Professor at Sorbonne University and Director of the Theoretical Chemistry Laboratory (Sorbonne University/CNRS), co-founder and Scientific Director of Qubit Pharmaceuticals.
Jean-Philip Piquemal, Professor at Sorbonne University and Director of the Theoretical Chemistry Laboratory (Sorbonne University/CNRS), co-founder and Scientific Director of Qubit Pharmaceuticals.

The quantum AI model, developed in partnership with Sorbonne University, is capable of modeling and simulating the behavior of molecules with a level of precision and computational speed never before achieved.

"This simulation method would reduce the cost of the drug discovery phase enormously," says Jean-Philip Piquemal, Professor at Sorbonne University and Director of the Theoretical Chemistry Laboratory (Sorbonne University/CNRS), co-founder and Scientific Director of Qubit Pharmaceuticals.

"This simulation method would reduce the cost of the drug discovery phase enormously," says Jean-Philip Piquemal, Professor at Sorbonne University and Director of the Theoretical Chemistry Laboratory (Sorbonne University/CNRS), co-founder and Scientific Director of Qubit Pharmaceuticals.
Extending Google DeepMind's AlphaFold revolution from chemistry to biology using synthetic data

interaction_phrase: the Sorbonne University research team that developed FeNNix-Bio1
occurrence_sentence: The Sorbonne University research team that developed FeNNix-Bio1 set itself the goal of going beyond the capabilities of AlphaFold, the artificial intelligence software developed by Google DeepMind, which provides a prediction of protein structure based on their amino acid sequence.
Extending Google DeepMind's AlphaFold revolution from chemistry to biology using synthetic data

interaction_phrase: AlphaFold, the artificial intelligence software developed by Google DeepMind
occurrence_sentence: The Sorbonne University research team that developed FeNNix-Bio1 set itself the goal of going beyond the capabilities of AlphaFold, the artificial intelligence software developed by Google DeepMind, which provides a prediction of protein structure based on their amino acid sequence.

The Sorbonne University research team that developed FeNNix-Bio1 set itself the goal of going beyond the capabilities of AlphaFold, the artificial intelligence software developed by Google DeepMind, which provides a prediction of protein structure based on their amino acid sequence.
"We're aiming at complex targets, those for which the pharmaceutical industry doesn't provide a solution for patients," comments Robert Marino, CEO of Qubit Pharmaceuticals.
Qubit Pharmaceuticals is already using quantum data to enrich its models – something long thought impossible until 2035, shares Jean-Philip Piquemal.

interaction_phrase: spin-off from the research work of five internationally renowned scientists
occurrence_sentence: Qubit Pharmaceuticals was founded in 2020 with the vision of co-developing new, safer and more effective drugs with pharmaceutical and biotech companies. A spin-off from the research work of five internationally renowned scientists - Louis Lagardère (Sorbonne University and CNRS), Matthieu Montes (CNAM), Jean-Philip Piquemal (Sorbonne University and CNRS), Jay Ponder (Washington University in St Louis), Pengyu Ren (University of Texas at Austin) - Qubit Pharmaceuticals leverages its Atlas platform to discover new drugs through simulation and molecular modeling accelerated by hybrid HPC and quantum computing.

interaction_phrase: appoints Robin Chaudret as Chief Technology Officer
occurrence_sentence: Qubit Pharmaceuticals announces the appointment of Robin Chaudret, a renowned expert in molecular modeling, as Chief Technology Officer

interaction_phrase: appoints Robin Chaudret, a renowned expert in molecular modeling, as Chief Technology Officer
occurrence_sentence: Qubit Pharmaceuticals announces the appointment of Robin Chaudret, a renowned expert in molecular modeling, as Chief Technology Officer.

His doctoral research focused on the multiscale modeling of cations (positive ions) of heavy metals and the development of polarizable force fields. His postdoctoral research includes studying enzymatic reaction mechanisms at Duke University in the United States and developing methods for in silico enzymatic engineering at IFP Energies Nouvelles. Robin Chaudret is a graduate of École Normale Supérieure (2009) and holds a PhD in computational chemistry from Pierre and Marie Curie University (UPMC) in Paris, co-supervised by Jean-Philip Piquemal, co-founder of Qubit Pharmaceuticals.

The multidisciplinary team, led by CEO Robert Marino, and the founders are based in France at the Paris Santé Cochin incubator and in the USA in Boston.

interaction_phrase: spin-off from the research work of
occurrence_sentence: Qubit Pharmaceuticals was founded in 2020 with the vision of co-developing new, safer and more effective drugs with pharmaceutical and biotech companies. A spin-off from the research work of five internationally renowned scientists - Louis Lagardère (Sorbonne University and CNRS), Matthieu Montes (CNAM), Jean-Philip Piquemal (Sorbonne University and CNRS), Jay Ponder (Washington University in St Louis), Pengyu Ren (University of Texas at Austin) - Qubit Pharmaceuticals leverages its Atlas platform to discover new drugs through simulation and molecular modeling accelerated by hybrid HPC and quantum computing.

A spin-off from the research work of five internationally renowned scientists - Louis Lagardère (Sorbonne University and CNRS), Matthieu Montes (CNAM), Jean-Philip Piquemal (Sorbonne University and CNRS), Jay Ponder (Washington University in St Louis), Pengyu Ren (University of Texas at Austin) - Qubit Pharmaceuticals leverages its Atlas platform to discover new drugs through simulation and molecular modeling accelerated by hybrid HPC and quantum computing.
The multidisciplinary team, led by CEO Robert Marino, and the founders are based in France at the Paris Santé Cochin incubator and in the USA in Boston.

Qubit Pharmaceuticals has been named "Technology Pioneer 2024" by the World Economic Forum, and has several high-profile partnerships such as the Institut Curie, Sorbonne University, and the Institute of Pharmacology at the University of Sherbrooke in Canada.

interaction_phrase: Cité de l'Innovation de Sorbonne Université
occurrence_sentence: The Sorbonne University selection committee has chosen the first five innovative companies to set up in the business centre of the Cité de l'Innovation de Sorbonne Université.
The Cité de l'Innovation business centre is intended to accommodate innovative research and development companies that work closely with Sorbonne Université

These start-ups, whose technologies originate from the university laboratories, have all presented, in their application and then during their hearing, a project of scientific cooperation and development of their relations with the student community of Sorbonne Université.
Construction began in September 2022 with an overall budget of 69 million euros, co-financed by the City of Paris, the State, the Île-de-France Region and Sorbonne Université.
